data.content:
Now I have the full picture. Let me create the `Directory.Packages.props` and update all project files.

**Version conflicts found:**
- `System.Text.Json`: 10.0.1 vs 8.0.4 → **10.0.1**
- `Azure.Identity`: 1.13.2 vs 1.11.4 → **1.13.2**

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Identity" Version="1.13.2" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I need to analyze the package versions across all projects and pick the highest version for each:

| Package | Api | Shared | Tests | Worker | Highest |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| System.Text.Json | 10.0.1 | 10.0.1 | 10.0.1 | 8.0.4 | 10.0.1 |
| Azure.Identity | 1.13.2 | 1.11.4 | - | - | 1.13.2 |
| xunit | - | - | 2.9.3 | - | 2.9.3 |


| Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting | - | - | - | 8.0.1 | 8.0.1 |
| OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting | - | - | - | 1.15.0 | 1.15.0 |

Now I'll create a `Directory.Packages.props` file at the solution root with centralized package version management enabled, listing all the highest versions I've identified, then remove the version attributes from the individual project files.

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Identity" Version="1.13.2" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.content:
Done. Here's what I did:

**Created** `Directory.Packages.props` with centralized versions for all 5 packages.

**Resolved 2 version conflicts** (highest wins):
- `System.Text.Json`: 8.0.4 → **10.0.1**
- `Azure.Identity`: 1.11.4 → **1.13.2**

**Updated 4 `.csproj` files** — removed `Version` attributes from all `PackageReference` elements so versions are managed centrally.
